figures.caption: Table 2. Key Pairwise Comparisons — Composite Score and Goal Alignment

Figure 2 (a–c). Mean cross-model standard deviation (σ) by condition. Lower σ indicates more consistent outputs across three LLMs. Blue: composite score; orange: goal alignment. Note: Condition A's low composite σ in ZH/JA is an inflation artifact (§5.3, §6.2).

Figure 1 (a–c). Mean composite score and goal alignment by condition across three languages. Error bars indicate ±1 SD. Exact values are reported in Table 1. Note: Condition A's high composite scores reflect baseline inflation (§6.2); GA is the more meaningful comparison.

Table 3. Cross-Model Standard Deviation by Condition and Language (Lower σ = more consistent outputs across models; bold = lowest σ per column)
Table 1. Mean Composite Score and Goal Alignment (mean ± SD, N=180 per cell; bold = highest value per column)
